_Make your brand stand out with a custom mobile app you don’t have to build yourself._

_With Branded Mobile by Thinkific, we'll build and maintain a white-labeled app for your online courses and communities so you can focus on what you do best._

[![](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/17440873557143)](https://thinkific.com/branded-mobile/?utm_source=knowledge_base&utm_medium=help_center&utm_campaign=BrandedMobile2023)

## In this article:

[About This Feature](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13166713617431-Maximize-Your-Brand-Potential-With-the-Branded-Mobile-App#about)

[Frequently Asked Questions](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13166713617431-Maximize-Your-Brand-Potential-With-the-Branded-Mobile-App#faq)

## About This Feature

Branded Mobile fits into your audience’s existing routines, making it easier for them to learn and engage with your content — all on their own schedule. This means higher course completion rates, improved student satisfaction, and more revenue opportunities for your business.

Make your app instantly recognizable and discoverable with a fully branded, white-labeled mobile solution that reflects — and highlights — your company’s identity across every channel.

> We're continuously working on improving the mobile app solution. Keep checking this article for more information on the latest releases: [Mobile App Recent Updates](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/14454992010135).

## How It Works

To start the process of Branded Mobile App enablement, you will need to complete the [steps outlined below.](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13166713617431-Maximize-Your-Brand-Potential-With-the-Branded-Mobile-App#getstarted) You will partner with the Thinkific team to help you build and publish your app.

Once your app is built, approved, and released, all your students need to do is download your app from the Google Play Store or Apple App Store.

After downloading and installing the app, your audience can access their learning products, participate in communal learning interactions, and enjoy fast, easy, on-the-go access to materials they need, all within the app that extends your brand identity across all learning channels.

Some of the features of the Branded Mobile App include:

- Your own Google Play Store and Apple App Store listings
- App with your own brand name, colors, app icon, and loading screen
- Automatic app updates
- In-app purchases
- Easy navigation
- Course overview and all lesson types
- Lesson completion and progress tracking
- Optimized Community content
- Seamless access to Community Spaces and activity feeds
- Posts and comments within Spaces
- Navigation between courses and Communities with the Course - Community connection
- In-app notification center for replies, comments, and @Mentions in Communities
- Drip release notifications to keep students informed when content becomes available

...and more!

To learn more about what features are currently available on the Mobile App, check out the [Features](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13166713617431-Maximize-Your-Brand-Potential-With-the-Branded-Mobile-App#features) section below.

> Looking for a mobile app just to get started and get up and running instantly? Check out [our Thinkific Mobile App](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/10664253689367)!

## Important Considerations

- The Branded Mobile App is designed to deliver amazing learning experiences to students and does not support school administration. Site Owners and Admins (and users with other roles other than Student) will need to use the web version of Thinkific.
- We're continuously working on improving the mobile app, but if you have feedback about what you'd like to see in the app, we want to know! [Get in contact with us](https://thinkific.com/contact/) and let us know what functionality would be most valuable to you!
- Keep checking this article for more information on the latest releases: [Mobile App Recent Updates](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/14454992010135).
- Please review the important considerations when it comes to the Branded Mobile app solution: [Branded Mobile: Important Considerations](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/14290873077911).

## Getting Started

> Please ensure that you complete the following **before** purchasing the app:
>
> - **Apply to receive a D-U-N-S number.** See more: [Branded Mobile: Why Do I Need an Apple Organization Account?](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/15163907994775)
> - **Set up your Apple / Google accounts**. Follow these instructions to set up your [Apple App Store](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/14510359514647) and [Google Play Store](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/14510318393111) accounts.
>
> It can take up to 30 days to receive a D-U-N-S number, which is required for the Apple and Google account setup. Setting up these accounts in advance greatly expedites the app building process.

You can purchase the Branded Mobile App add-on and get started by following these steps:

1. From your Admin Dashboard, select **Channels**
2. Select **Mobile App**
3. Click on **Your Branded App** tab
4. Select the desired billing option (monthly or annual)

![](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/18800593442967)
5. After selecting a monthly or an annual option, confirm your payment details and review your order
6. **Accept** Terms of Service and select **Purchase**


> You will be charged for the Branded Mobile App add-on right away.

7. Once confirmed, you will see the list of required tasks. You need to complete these tasks before our team can start building the app:
1. **Get a D-U-N-S number,** if you have not done so already. [Request your D-U-N-S number here](https://www.dnb.com/ca-en/duns-number/get-a-duns.html).
2. **Set up your Apple / Google accounts**, if you have not done so already. Follow these instructions to set up your [Apple App Store](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/14510359514647) and [Google Play Store](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/14510318393111) accounts.
3. **Fill out your information and connect your accounts**. Fill out your personal and App Store / Play Store listings information by navigating to **Channels > Mobile App > Your Branded App > App Information**
4. Connect your Apple App Store and Google Play Store accounts by navigating to **Channels > Mobile App > Your Branded App > Connect** and supplying the necessary keys
8. _(Optional)_ Customize your app with your choice of primary colors, text colors, button styles, and more to suit your design preferences: [Branded Mobile: How can I customize my branded app colors?](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/18806564986647)
9. Once you complete all of the required steps, click **Build My App**

Our team will start building your app and will be in touch with further instructions.

## Features

The Branded Mobile App allows your audience to access many of the features that are available to them via the web as well. See the list of support and unsupported features below:

### Branded Mobile-Only Features

The Branded Mobile app comes with unique features that allow you to differentiate your business. These features include:

- **Your own Google Play Store and Apple App Store listings**: This makes it easy for your audience to discover your branded mobile app while giving you the flexibility and control to personalize your listing to reflect your brand.
- **Your own app**: App with your own brand name, colors, app icon, and loading screen. Customize your app with your choice of primary colors, text colors, button styles, and more to suit your design preferences.
- **Automatic app updates**: When new app versions are created, they will automatically be submitted for review and pushed live to your app store listings on your behalf - saving you time and getting the latest and greatest app version in your audience’s hands faster than ever.
- **In-App Purchases**: Take your audience’s learning journey to new heights! Your users can now expand their skills by purchasing new products directly within your mobile app. With Apple and Google Play’s in-app purchase systems, they can easily tap, purchase, and get access to new products. Learn more: [Branded Mobile: In-App Purchases](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/16261125243159).

To learn more, see the following article: [Branded Mobile-Only Features](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13489267385111#branded).

### Mobile-Only Features

Both Branded and Thinkific Mobile Apps offer some unique features that are available only on a mobile. These features include:

- [Drip Lessons](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030741033) release notifications: students can now receive notifications on their mobile app letting them know when previously locked lessons are available!
- [Push notifications for community activity](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13489201576983#supported): draw students back in and keep engagement high with the new push notifications. Students get notified when new activity happens in the communities they are enrolled in.

To learn more, see the following article: [Mobile-Only Features](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13489267385111).

### Course Features

Course features on the mobile app allow your students to access your courses, anytime, anywhere.

The app is considered an additive complement to the web experience. As such, we have prioritized providing end users with lesson types that will allow them to complete learning on the go, and access your content in the most convenient way for them.

Below, you will find a list of what features are currently available on the Mobile App, and what features are not supported at this time.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| **Features that are supported for Mobile** | **Features that are not currently supported** |
| Lesson Type: Text | Apps: Any Thinkific App Store apps |
| Lesson Type: Video | Feature: On-lesson discussions |
| Lesson Type: Audio | Setting: Free preview |
| Lesson Type: Download | Setting: Access to the content offline |
| Lesson Type: PDF | Setting: Course Player appearance (individual course player customizations, ability to hide lesson type and icon) |
| Lesson Type: Multimedia | Setting: Course Player behavior customizations (disabling text copying, social sharing, customizing chapter and course completion messages, disabling chapter completion messages) |
| Lesson Type: Presentation | Feature: Disqus Discussion Forum |
| Lesson Type: Live | Feature: Course Reviews |
| Lesson Type: Assessment Lessons (Quizzes, Surveys, Assignments, Brillium Exams) |  |
| Setting: Prerequisite Lesson |  |
| Setting: Drip Schedule |  |
| Feature: Default chapter completion message |  |
| Feature: [SCORM-Compliant courses](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/24607412212119) |  |
| Feature: [Thinker, AI Teaching Assistant](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/34681678535191) |  |

To learn more, see the following article: [Supported Course Features](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/10113800644631).

### Community Features

Most of the community features are supported on the Mobile App. Below, you will find a list of what features are currently available on the Mobile App and what features are not supported at this time.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| **Features that are supported for Mobile** | **Features that are not currently supported** |
| Feature: Spaces | Feature: Posts pinning |
| Feature: Post Creation | Feature: Community Moderation |
| Feature: Comments and replies |  |
| Feature: Post and comment editing |  |
| Feature: Posts and threads following |  |
| Feature: Course-Community connection |  |
| Feature: Reactions |  |
| Feature: Mentions |  |
| Feature: Community Activity Notifications |  |
| Feature: Direct Messages |  |
| Feature: Profiles and Member directory |  |
| Feature: Community Live Events |  |
| Feature: Multimedia in posts: multiple image and file uploads, video uploads directly from the device |  |

To learn more, see the following article: [Support Community Features](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13489201576983).

### General Features

In addition to Course and Community features, the app supports the general features outlined below.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| **Features that are supported for Mobile** | **Features that are not currently supported** |
| Feature: Digital Download Products | Feature: Offline support |
| Feature: Coaching & Webinar Products | Setting: Password change |
| Feature: In-App Notifications | Feature: Customizable Push Notifications |
| Feature: Privacy Policy and Terms of Use |  |
| Feature: Product Search |  |
| Feature: Availability in multiple languages\* |  |
| Feature: Certificates |  |
| Setting: Profile and profile editing |  |
| Setting: Access to the school support email address |  |
| Setting: Notifications |  |
| Setting: Billing |  |
| Feature: In-App Purchases ( _Branded app only)_ |  |
| Feature: In-App Purchases Subscription management ( _Branded app only)_ |  |

_\*To see the list of languages that the app is available in, see:_ [_Mobile App FAQ - Frequently Asked Questions_](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/10114502955671) _._

To learn more, see the following article: [Supported General Features](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/13489247839127).

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Do I need to manually update my Branded app when Thinkific releases a new feature?**

No, all mobile apps will be updated remotely by Thinkific.

**Can I create learning products or manage my site using the app?**

No, the Branded Mobile App does not support school administration. Site Owners and Admins (and users with other roles other than Student) will need to use the web version of Thinkific.

**Does the Branded Mobile app work everywhere or are there location limitations?**

There are no regional access limitations. We are global, so the Thinkific Mobile app works everywhere Thinkific does.

**What are the operating system requirements?**

The Branded Mobile app has the same requirements as the Thinkific Mobile app. For mobile device operating system requirements, see: [OS (Operating System) Requirements for the Thinkific Mobile App](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/10094941410327).

**Can my students access content offline?**

No, at the moment, the Thinkific Mobile App does not support the delivery of content offline. You can allow your students to download certain lesson content (videos, PDFs, audio) and they can view it directly on their devices.

We are working hard on bringing the ability to access content offline to the mobile app! Stay tuned for more updates: [Mobile App Recent Updates](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/14454992010135).

For more questions, see: [Mobile App: FAQ - Frequently Asked Questions](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/10114502955671)

[Next article Prepare Your iOS Branded Mobile App](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/14510359514647-Prepare-Your-iOS-Branded-Mobile-App)

### The Branded Mobile advantage

Elevate your business and boost revenue with a mobile app for your learning experiences — no technical expertise required.

#### Earn more with in-app purchases

Our in-app purchase feature lets your audience easily buy additional courses and continue their learning journey — all without leaving your app.

![Branded Mobile in-app purchase preview](https://www.thinkific.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/brandedmobile-features-1.png)

![Thinkific expert working](https://www.thinkific.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/brandedmobile-features-2.png)

#### Let our trusted experts bring your app to life

After you purchase and complete a short form about your business, our knowledgeable team members will get started on your branded mobile app and schedule a one-on-one call with you.

#### Enhance your brand presence

Increase visibility and recognition across all platforms with an app that puts the spotlight on your one-of-a-kind brand. With a personalized app store listing, your app is also more discoverable to your learners.

![screenshots of branded mobile experience](https://www.thinkific.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/brandedmobile-features-3.png)

![Mobile communities screen](https://www.thinkific.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/brandedmobile-features-4.png)

#### Courses and communities at their fingertips — at any time

With features like push notifications, on-the-go access, and activity feeds right in their pocket, your audience can stay connected to your content and their peers at all times.

I’ve been using Thinkific basically since it came out. About 13 years now. It’s our primary learning management system. I use it for all of our courses.

Travis Bader

Silvercore Outdoors

![](https://www.thinkific.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/testimonial-TarvisBader-1.png)

## Frequently asked questions

►

How do I get the mobile app?

**For Existing Customers:** You can activate the app [directly from your admin.](https://courses.thinkific.com/onboarding/signin?redirect_to=/manage/mobile)

1. Log in to your Thinkific Admin area.
2. In the left-hand menu, navigate to **Channels**.
3. Click on **Mobile App** and follow the prompts to activate your branded mobile experience.

**For New Customers:** You will need a Thinkific account to launch your app. First, **[start your free trial](https://courses.thinkific.com/signup?plan=low_22&interval=month)** to set up your account, and then you can access the Mobile App channel from your admin.

►

How much does the mobile app cost?

The Branded Mobile App is an add-on to your current Thinkific subscription and costs **$199 USD/month**.

**Want to save on your subscription?** If you choose to pay for your mobile app annually, you will receive a **10%** discount on the app’s overall cost.

**For Existing Customers:** [You can activate the app directly from your admin.](https://courses.thinkific.com/onboarding/signin?redirect_to=/manage/mobile)

►

Can learners do everything on the app without going on the web version?

The app is considered an additive complement to the web experience. Learners can use the app to learn on the go and access creators’ content in the most convenient way for them. This includes accessing lessons, interacting in communities, staying up-to-date with push notifications, and enrolling in or purchasing new products.

►

Will my learners be able to access both my courses and communities on the app?

Yes, your learners will be able to access both [communities](https://www.thinkific.com/features/learning-communities/) and [courses](https://www.thinkific.com/features/courses/) on your mobile app.

►

Can my learners download files from lessons?

Yes! Both downloads and PDF lesson types are available on Branded Mobile. You can also allow your students to download certain lesson content (videos, PDFs and audio) and they can view it directly on their device.

►

If I update or add new content, will it be reflected in my mobile app?

Yes! However, you won’t be able to make any changes from within the app. Any changes you make must be done on the web (browser) version of your site, and they will then be reflected for your audience in the mobile app.

►

What can I customize with my own branded mobile app?

With Branded Mobile, you can customize your app to reflect your company’s brand identity, including the imagery and descriptions on the Google Play and Apple App Store listings. Additionally, you can customize your app name, app icon, and load screen. From within the app, you can choose your app’s primary colours, button styles and text colours.

►

How much maintenance will I need for the app? Who will supply updates, files or patches?

You control your app store listing’s look and feel and can respond to reviews, while our team of experts will handle all technical maintenance and ongoing improvements to keep your app running smoothly.

## Ready to grow revenue with a mobile app?

# Eight Best White-Label LMS Platforms to Customize Learning in 2026

- Mar 20, 2026

![](https://cdn.shortpixel.ai/spai/q_lossy+to_auto+ret_img/www.d2l.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/T025EV233-U0344A1SJAK-666652b2f605-512-150x150.jpg)

![](https://www.d2l.com/blog/white-label-lms/)

If you’re evaluating white-label LMS platforms for 2026, you already know the difference between surface-level rebranding and genuine white-label architecture. You need a platform that supports brand-consistent learning across regions and audiences, multi-portal management and custom domain control, not just a logo swap on a vendor’s subdomain.

This guide cuts through vague “white-label” claims. We’ll walk you through a practical integrity framework and a demo verification checklist so you can pressure-test vendor promises before you sign anything.

~~~~

### Brightspace

A white-label LMS can decide whether your learning experience stays consistent across domains, portals and programs as you scale. D2L Brightspace supports enterprise branding, multi-portal governance and analytics you can trust.

[Explore Brightspace >](https://www.d2l.com/brightspace/)

## The Best White-Label LMS Platforms for 2026

Enterprises can’t settle for white-label platforms that offer surface-level branding: logic, colors, maybe a custom domain. Your branded learning platform must allow customization at these four aspects:

1. Brand (themes, terminology, portal-level branding)
2. Access (custom domains, SSO flows, redirects)
3. Comms (emails, certificates, notifications)
4. Governance (multi-portal support, delegated admin, reporting boundaries)

This is the framework we used to build our shortlist. It’s also consistent with how analysts like Forrester [evaluate enterprise LMS platforms](https://www.d2l.com/en-apac/blog/the-complete-lms-evaluation-guide/).

[The Forrester Wave: Learning Management Systems and Experience Platforms, Q4 2025](https://www.forrester.com/report/the-forrester-wave-tm-learning-management-systems-a%20nd-experience-platforms-q4-2025/RES188058), moves well beyond feature checklists; it scores vendors on enterprise support, governance controls, AI oversight and connection to business outcomes. We used the same lens.

Beyond branding depth, we also assessed scalability, third-party integrations, learning analytics, mobile customization and how well each platform supports long-term enterprise growth.

If you’re early in your search, start with our complete LMS evaluation guide. It’ll help you [align your learning strategy with business goals](https://www.d2l.com/blog/3-reasons-to-align-learning-strategy-with-business-goals) before vendor demos pull your attention toward cosmetic features.

Here are the top contenders at a glance:

## D2L Brightspace

[Brightspace](https://www.d2l.com/brightspace/) is a cloud-based learning platform built for organizations that need structured, scalable learning with genuine white-label depth. It goes further than most platforms on brand governance: not just theming, but portal-level isolation, delegated administration and analytics that leadership will actually use.

For enterprise L&D and HR teams managing multiple audiences, regions or business units, Brightspace is designed to hold consistent branding at every touchpoint: from the login page to the certificate in a learner’s inbox.

### Key Features

- Portal-level branding with theme controls, terminology customization and logo management
- Custom domain support and SSO configuration via SAML 2.0, Azure AD and Okta
- Branded email templates, notification controls and custom certificate design
- Multi-portal architecture with delegated admin roles and reporting boundaries
- Advanced analytics dashboards via Performance+ with data export for leadership reporting
- D2L Link for HRIS, CRM and collaboration tool integrations
- Mobile app with branded learner experience

### Pros

- Branding holds at portal level, not just globally, critical for multi-audience deployments
- Analytics go beyond completion rates: you can tie learning activity to [corporate learning analytics](https://www.d2l.com/blog/data-analytics-in-corporate-learning/) and business outcomes
- Delegated admin model gives regional teams control without breaking central governance
- [D2L for Business](https://www.d2l.com/blog/how-d2l-for-business-solves-organizational-challenges/) supports the full range of enterprise use cases: onboarding, compliance, upskilling and enablement
- Strong [accessible education](https://www.d2l.com/blog/accessible-education-the-complete-guide/) standards built into the platform

### Limitations

- Full white-label depth (custom mobile app, advanced comms branding) may require configuration support

### Best For

Enterprise organizations that need brand-consistent learning across audiences without sacrificing analytics, governance and integration readiness.

~~~~

### Contact Sales

Run the same demo checks across every vendor and you’ll spot branding gaps fast. If you want to see what end-to-end white-labeling looks like in practice, take a quick product walkthrough with Brightspace.

[Talk to our team >](https://www.d2l.com/contact-sales/)

## Cornerstone

[Cornerstone](https://www.d2l.com/blog/cornerstone-ondemand-competitors/) is a talent management platform with a strong LMS component, built for large enterprises that want learning tightly connected to performance management, [compliance tracking](https://www.cornerstoneondemand.com/solutions/compliance-management/) and skills development. Its white-label capabilities are solid [at an enterprise level](https://www.cornerstoneondemand.com/platform/extended-enterprise-training/), particularly around governance and reporting, though the learner interface is less polished than some alternatives on this list.

### Key Features

- Multi-audience learning delivery across global teams and extended enterprise
- Skill-based learning paths with automated certificate issuance
- Custom reporting and [advanced analytics across programs](https://www.cornerstoneondemand.com/solutions/analytics-and-reporting/)
- Social learning and AI-driven personalization
- Deep integration with HR performance and talent systems

### Pros

- [Reporting and compliance](https://www.g2.com/products/cornerstone-learning/reviews?source=search) capabilities are well-rated
- Governance model supports complex organizational hierarchies
- Reliable for organizations already running Cornerstone for talent management

### Limitations

- Interface can feel dated compared to lighter-weight competitors
- Higher price point and branding customization requires more configuration effort
- Less suited to orgs where learner experience is the primary brand priority

### Best For

Large enterprises where compliance depth, analytics and HR system integration are higher priorities than learner-facing brand flexibility.

## TalentLMS

[TalentLMS](https://www.d2l.com/blog/talentlms-alternatives/) is built for speed and simplicity. It’s one of the fastest platforms to deploy on this list, with a clean learner interface and branding options that are easy to configure without technical support. It’s a popular choice for SMBs and fast-growing teams that need a white-label-ready platform without enterprise complexity.

### Key Features

- Theme and logo customization at account level
- Custom domain and subdomain support
- [SCORM/xAPI compliance](https://www.talentlms.com/features/scorm-lms) for structured course delivery
- eCommerce with PayPal and Stripe; [HR tool integrations](https://www.talentlms.com/integrations)
- [Mobile access](https://www.talentlms.com/mobile) and discussion forums

### Pros

- Fast to set up
- Branding configuration is straightforward and doesn’t require developer support
- Intuitive learner interface minimizes onboarding friction

### Limitations

- Branding is account-level, not portal-level (not designed for multi-brand deployments)
- [Limited advanced analytics](https://www.g2.com/products/talentlms/reviews?source=search) and custom reporting
- May be outgrown quickly as programs scale in complexity

### Best For

SMBs and budget-conscious organizations that need quick deployment, easy brand customization and reliable day-to-day usability.

## Absorb LMS

[Absorb LMS](https://www.d2l.com/blog/absorb-lms-alternatives/) is a user-friendly platform that combines clean design, strong automation and built-in eCommerce. It’s a natural fit for mid-sized organizations that want polished white-label delivery without a steep implementation curve. Its multi-portal support is more capable than many SMB-tier platforms, making it a genuine option for organizations managing more than one learner audience.

### Key Features

- Multi-portal support for segmented learner groups
- CE tracking with [automated certificate issuance](https://www.absorblms.com/features/smart-administration)
- [API integrations](https://www.absorblms.com/features/integrations) and built-in [eCommerce tools](https://www.absorblms.com/features/ecommerce)
- Standard reporting dashboards for completions and progress
- Custom branding at portal level

### Pros

- [Clean and simple interface](https://www.g2.com/products/absorb-software-absorb-lms/reviews?source=search#reviews)
- Fast to implement without a large technical team
- Portal-level branding works well for organizations with distinct audience segments

### Limitations

- Advanced analytics may require additional modules
- Customization depth is more limited than enterprise-tier platforms
- Governance model isn’t designed for highly complex multi-region deployments

### Best For

Mid-sized organizations that need strong usability, automated workflows and white-label delivery across multiple learner groups.

## 360Learning

[360Learning](https://www.d2l.com/blog/360learning-alternatives/) blends LMS and learning experience platform (LXP) capabilities into an AI-powered platform built for collaborative learning. It’s designed for organizations where subject matter experts inside your business contribute content alongside structured programs. Its white-label features are solid for branded delivery, though its governance model is less suited to complex multi-portal enterprise needs.

### Key Features

- [AI-driven enrollment and personalization](https://360learning.com/product/artificial-intelligence/)
- Mandatory training support with [certificate issuance](https://360learning.com/product/learning-management-system/#certificate-expiration-alerts)
- Dashboards tracking engagement, progress and completion
- [HRIS, Slack and Teams integrations](https://360learning.com/partner-marketplace/)
- Branded learning environment with custom domain support

### Pros

- Enables internal subject matter experts to build and contribute content
- AI features reduce administrative overhead as programs scale
- Strong engagement and social learning tools

### Limitations

- White-label governance depth is limited compared to enterprise-tier platforms
- It might require [more complex configuration](https://www.g2.com/products/360learning/reviews?source=search)
- Higher price point relative to comparable mid-market options

### Best For

Mid-to-large organizations that prioritize collaborative, AI-driven learning with strong engagement and peer content creation.

## LearnUpon

[LearnUpon](https://www.d2l.com/blog/learnupon-alternatives/) is designed to serve multiple audiences: employees, partners and customers. It does this from a single environment and is a good fit for organizations that need to scale quickly across a diverse learner base while maintaining portal-level brand separation. Its setup is straightforward and its integration library is broad.

### Key Features

- Cloud-based multi-portal architecture
- Built-in certification and credential tracking
- Performance, progress and completion [tracked in dashboards](https://www.learnupon.com/features/reporting/)
- Custom domain support and branded learner portals
- HRIS, eCommerce and CRM platform [integrations](https://www.learnupon.com/features/)

### Pros

- Multi-portal model supports genuine brand isolation between audiences
- Broad integration library covers most HRIS and CRM use cases
- Consistent [customer support](https://www.g2.com/products/learnupon-lms/reviews?source=search) during and after onboarding

### Limitations

- Limited community and social learning features
- Customization options are more constrained than enterprise platforms
- Analytics are functional but not leadership-grade out of the box

### Best For

Mid-to-large organizations seeking centralized, scalable learning delivery across varied audiences with clear portal separation.

## iSpring Learn

iSpring Learn (also called [iSpring LMS](https://www.ispring.com/)) is a corporate LMS with genuine white-label depth across branding, mobile and comms and a standout integration with iSpring Suite, its PowerPoint-based authoring tool. The two products work natively together, making it a strong option for organizations that produce a lot of in-house training content and want a seamless authoring-to-delivery workflow.

### Key Features

- Custom domain, branded portal and login page with full logo and color scheme control
- Branded [native iOS and Android app](https://www.ispring.com/features): custom name, icon and app store listing, with iSpring handling technical maintenance
- SCORM 1.2/2004, xAPI, cmi5 and AICC [compliance](https://www.ispring.com/solutions/compliance-training-lms)
- [Integrations with HR systems](https://www.ispring.com/integrations), CRMs, video conferencing tools (Zoom, Teams) and eCommerce platforms
- Gamification: badges, points and leaderboards

### Pros

- [Ease of use](https://www.g2.com/products/ispring-lms/reviews?source=search) for content teams
- Sub-portal model supports multi-audience and multi-department deployments without significant admin overhead
- Straightforward to set up and configure, even without a dedicated technical team

### Limitations

- Multi-portal governance depth is less robust than enterprise-tier platforms — delegation and reporting boundary controls are more limited
- Analytics are functional but don’t reach the leadership-grade depth of platforms like Brightspace or Cornerstone
- Pricing can increase notably as user numbers scale

### Best For

Mid-sized organizations that need solid white-label coverage, a branded mobile app and clean course delivery, particularly where in-house content creation is part of the workflow.

## Thinkific Plus

Thinkific Plus is built for businesses that sell or distribute learning as a product. Its white-label features are genuinely strong on the learner-facing side (custom domains, branded storefronts, a dedicated branded mobile app and a clean course environment), making it a solid fit for customer education, partner training and course-led business models.

For internal L&D programs with compliance and governance requirements, it’s less suited.

### Key Features

- Custom domain and fully branded course environment
- SSO via OpenID Connect for [integrated brand experience](https://www.thinkific.com/features/branded-mobile/)
- White-label storefront with flexible pricing, subscriptions and group access controls
- [Custom analytics dashboards](https://www.thinkific.com/features/analytics/) covering enrollment, revenue, engagement and marketing performance (Advanced Analytics available as add-on)
- Integrations with marketing, CRM and eCommerce tools

### Pros

- Branded storefront experience for external learners
- Easy to set up and manage without a technical team
- Flexible monetization options for [course-led businesses](https://www.thinkific.com/features/courses/)

### Limitations

- Enterprise governance (multi-portal admin, delegated roles, reporting boundaries) [is limited](https://www.g2.com/products/thinkific/reviews?source=search)
- Analytics are lightweight for leadership-level reporting
- Not designed for complex internal L&D programs with compliance requirements

### Best For

Course-led businesses and customer education teams that need a polished branded storefront and flexible learner experience.

## A Buyer’s Guide To Choosing A White-Label LMS

Now that you’ve seen how the top platforms compare, here’s how to build an evaluation process that holds up beyond the demo.

### Step 1: Clarify What “White-Label” Must Cover in Your Org

The most common buying mistake? Asking vendors about logo, colors and domain  and discovering post-purchase that emails still come from a vendor domain, the mobile app isn’t brandable and multi-portal governance doesn’t actually exist.

Before you build a shortlist, map your needs to the White-Label Integrity Stack. Pick the two surfaces that matter most for your use case right now. If you’re running learner-facing programs for external partners, Access and Comms will matter most. If you’re scaling across business units, Governance is non-negotiable.

Being clear on this upfront also helps you focus [upskilling investment](https://www.d2l.com/blog/learning2030-making-upskilling-easier/) on the right platform architecture, not just the right feature list.

### Step 2: Define Your Non-Negotiables (HR/L&D + IT Together)

White-label LMS decisions rarely go wrong on features. They go wrong on integration and identity gaps that no one spotted before signing.

Pull your HR/L&D and IT leads into the same room and get aligned on:

- **Identity and access** — SSO provider, entry points, provisioning rules and fallback login behavior
- **Integrations** — which HRIS, CRM or collaboration tools need to connect and how data flows between them
- **Reporting** — who needs what dashboards and in what format
- **Scalability** — how many portals, regions and learner audiences you’ll need to support in 12–24 months

The business case matters here too.

According to [LinkedIn’s 2025 Workplace Learning Report](https://learning.linkedin.com/resources/workplace-learning-report), 88% of organizations cite retention as a top concern and providing learning opportunities is the number-one retention strategy. Only 36% of organizations qualify as genuine career development champions with programs tied to business outcomes.

That gap is exactly where a well-governed, brand-consistent learning platform creates a competitive advantage.

### Step 3: Validate the Learner Experience End-to-End

Understanding a platform’s features is one thing. Walking through the actual learner journey is another.

In your demo, run the full flow:

_invitation email → login → course discovery → learning path → completion → certificate → re-engagement_

This surfaces branding gaps that no feature list will reveal.

Pay attention to every touchpoint in the Access and Comms surfaces. Is the invitation email branded? Does the login page reflect your domain? What does the error page say if SSO fails?

[Design thinking in L&D](https://www.d2l.com/blog/design-thinking-in-learning-and-development-programs/) tells us that friction in the learner experience isn’t just a UX problem: it drives disengagement and non-completion. A branded, seamless journey isn’t cosmetic. It’s operational.

### Step 4: Confirm Analytics and Reporting Leaders Will Trust

Your LMS reporting needs to work for the people making decisions, not just the people running the platform.

Define “leadership-ready” before you enter a demo. That means adoption rates, completion trends, skills signals, program impact data and the ability to export or API-connect for cross-system reporting.

Ask vendors to show you these reports live, not screenshots. Confirm that export formats work with your existing BI tools and that reporting boundaries can be set at portal level for governance compliance.

For a deeper look at what good reporting looks like at the enterprise level, see our guide on [data analytics in corporate learning](https://www.d2l.com/blog/data-analytics-in-corporate-learning/).

## What to Look for in a White-Label LMS Platform

Use this section as your demo script.

For each surface, ask vendors to show you (not tell you) how it works.

### Brand Surface (Theme Controls, Terminology, Portal-Level Branding)

Ask the vendor to show:

- Logo, color, font and UI label changes made live in the demo — not pre-configured
- Platform terminology replaced at portal level (for example: “Academy” → “Learning Hub”)
- Branding applied to a second portal with different settings
- Whether CSS/custom themes are supported and how upgrades affect them

If branding is global-only with no portal-level control or terminology changes require vendor services or custom code, that might be a red flag to watch out for.

### Access Surface (Custom Domain/Subdomain, SSO Entry Points, Redirects, Error States)

**Ask the vendor to show:**

- The full login journey on a custom domain, live
- SSO flow end-to-end (IdP to LMS to branded landing page)
- What happens when SSO fails (error pages, fallback login options)
- Redirect rules between portals or regions

When a custom domain is “possible” but not standard or error pages display vendor branding or confusing technical messages, consider another vendor.

### Comms Surface (Emails, Notifications, Certificates, Sender Domains)

**Ask the vendor to show:**

- Branded email templates for invitations, password resets and reminders
- Sender domain control: confirm SPF/DKIM/DMARC readiness
- Notification controls: who receives what and when
- Branded certificates with logo, organization name and credential IDs

Red flags to watch out for include emails that come from a vendor domain by default with limited control and generic certificates that require manual design work outside the LMS.

### Mobile Surface (What Is Actually Brandable in Mobile)

**Ask the vendor to show:**

- The mobile learner experience in full: navigation, catalog, offline support
- What’s brandable inside the mobile app (logo, colors, name)
- Whether a fully branded mobile app is available and what’s included vs. paid
- The app release and update model (who owns publishing and approvals)

If the vendor claims “mobile is supported,” but branding is browser-only or if a branded app exists but requires a separate project with long lead times, the vendor might not make your shortlist.

### Governance Surface (Multi-Portal Setup, Delegated Admin, Role Isolation, Brand Isolation)

**Ask the vendor to show:**

- Two portals created with different branding and catalogs in the same environment
- Delegated admin roles: what a regional admin can and can’t do
- Brand isolation: confirm there’s no cross-portal leakage of assets, emails or catalog
- Reporting boundaries: portal-level analytics vs. global rollups

Consider it a red flag if the multi-portal is a workaround (not a real admin model) or the roles are too coarse, forcing central admin bottlenecks. If you’re planning to introduce [new learning programs](https://www.d2l.com/blog/why-a-busy-time-is-the-best-time-to-try-new-learning-programs/) alongside a platform migration, governance clarity upfront will save significant time later.

This is also where AI governance matters more than most buyers realize.

According to [McKinsey’s 2025 AI in the workplace research](https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/tech-and-ai/our-insights/superagency-in-the-workplace-empowering-people-to-unlock-ais-full-potential-at-work), despite near-universal AI investment, only 1% of companies describe themselves as having AI fully integrated into workflows. And 41% of employees are more apprehensive about AI than leadership assumes.

A platform’s AI features are only as useful as the governance controls around them, including audit trails, admin oversight and the ability to set boundaries by portal or role.

For a deeper look at how AI is reshaping enterprise learning, see our analysis of [AI in corporate learning and development](https://www.d2l.com/blog/signals-in-learning-how-ai-is-reshaping-corporate-learning-and-talent-development/) and our guide on [AI in learning strategy](https://www.d2l.com/blog/signals-in-learning-ten-best-uses-of-ai-in-creating-a-modern-learning-strategy/).

## Choose the Best White-Label LMS for Your Team

White-labeling isn’t a single feature. For enterprise organizations, it only works when branding holds across access, comms, mobile and governance, not just the UI.

Make your decision based on what you saw, not what vendors told you. Your goal is a branded learning platform that feels like part of your organization, not a bolt-on tool.

Compare [Brightspace](https://www.d2l.com/brightspace/) against your requirements for access, comms, mobile, governance and reporting and make the decision with confidence.

~~~~

### Request a customized demo of Brightspace

Your goal is a branded learning platform that feels like part of your organization, not a bolt-on tool. Compare Brightspace against your requirements for access, comms, mobile, governance and reporting and make the decision with confidence.

[Request a demo >](https://www.d2l.com/demo-request/email-demo/)

## Frequently Asked Questions About White-Label LMS

### What Is a White-Label LMS?

A white-label learning management system (LMS) is a platform you can brand as your own: with your organization’s name, domain, visual identity and communications, so learners never see the underlying vendor. True white-labeling goes beyond logo placement. It covers your custom domain, your branded emails and certificates, your mobile experience and your admin governance model.

### What White-Label LMS Features Matter Most for Enterprises?

For enterprise organizations, the five surfaces that matter most are brand (portal-level theme control and terminology customization), access (custom domain and SSO configuration), comms (branded emails, notifications and certificates), mobile (a brandable app experience) and governance (multi-portal support, delegated admin and reporting boundaries).

### What’s the Difference Between White-Labeling and Basic LMS Branding Options?

Basic branding means you can add a logo and change a color palette. White-labeling means your learners have no touchpoint with the vendor’s identity, from the login URL to the certificate they download. The difference becomes critical when you’re managing multiple audiences.

### How Do We Evaluate AI Features in a White-Label LMS Without Getting Distracted?

Focus on governance before capability. Ask vendors: Can you control which AI features are visible or active per portal? Do admins have audit trails for AI-generated content? Can you set boundaries for AI behavior by role or region? AI features that can’t be governed at portal level create brand and compliance risk in multi-audience environments.

### HWhat’s a Realistic Timeline to Migrate to a New White-Label LMS?

Most enterprise migrations run between three and nine months, depending on content volume, integration complexity and the number of portals you need to configure. The variables that most often extend timelines are SSO configuration (especially if multiple identity providers are involved), HRIS integration testing and branded comms setup across portals.

_With Branded Mobile by Thinkific, we'll build and maintain a white-labeled app for your online courses and communities so you can focus on what you do best. This article will outline some key considerations when it comes to the Branded Mobile app solution._

[![](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/17440963185559)](https://thinkific.com/branded-mobile/?utm_source=knowledge_base&utm_medium=help_center&utm_campaign=BrandedMobile2023)

## In this article:

[About This Feature](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/14290873077911-Branded-Mobile-Important-Considerations#about)

[In-App Payments Requirements](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/14290873077911-Branded-Mobile-Important-Considerations#iapreqs)

[Apple SSO and Account Duplication](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/14290873077911-Branded-Mobile-Important-Considerations#applesso)

[Frequently Asked Questions](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/14290873077911-Branded-Mobile-Important-Considerations#faq)

## About This Feature

Increase brand visibility and recognition with a white-labeled mobile app that has your own logo, colors, and style for a continuous experience across all channels.

A branded mobile app will fit into your audience’s existing routines, making it easier for them to learn and engage with your content — all on their own schedule. This means higher course completion rates, improved student satisfaction, and more revenue opportunities for your business.

There are some key considerations that you need to be aware of when it comes to the Branded Mobile solution.

> This article will continue to be updated, so keep checking back for the updated list of considerations.

## In-App Payments Requirements

Apple [requires](https://developer.apple.com/app-store/review/guidelines/#payments) that any digital goods (such as paid Thinkific courses) purchasable outside of the Apple App Store must also be available to purchase within the branded mobile app using [in-app purchases](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/16261125243159). If your account includes paid courses, it is likely to be flagged during the review process.

We recommend that you provide us with one or more courses for in-app purchase from the start to ensure that the Apple review process proceeds smoothly. This can be an existing course that you offer, or maybe a low-cost "taster" course or resources guide, etc.

Note that Apple may waive the requirement for in-app purchases for a few reasons. If you believe you qualify for any of the following exemptions, please let Thinkific know as quickly as possible:

- Your courses are free of charge
- Your courses are sold primarily to businesses (b2b), who make courses available to the end consumer (for details, see [here](https://developer.apple.com/app-store/review/guidelines/#enterprise-services))
- Your courses are bundled with physical goods or services (b2c), such as courses bundled with textbooks or courses offered as extended learning from a conference (for details, see [here](https://developer.apple.com/app-store/review/guidelines/#goods-and-services-outside-of-the-app))

## Apple SSO and Account Duplication

Your branded mobile app built by Thinkific needs to support the Apple SSO (Single Sign On) option, as required by Apple. This gives students to ability to log in to your site using their Apple ID.

When signing in to your site using the Apple sign-in option, your students will see two options: _Share My Email_ and _Hide My Email_. We recommend that your students select the option that they initially used when setting up their account. Learn more here: [What is Sign in with Apple?](https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT210318)

For students who previously used their actual email or the _Share My Email_ option, we recommend that they **do not** choose _Hide My Email_. Choosing this option while logging in, will create a brand new account meaning that the student will not be able to access their existing account.

To mitigate this, encourage your students to use the same login option they originally used when signing up on your site.

If a student has created a duplicate account, you can delete the duplicate user by following these instructions: [How to Delete a User](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360036378154).

> If a student registers on your site using Apple Sign in option, they will be registered as Apple User, due to the way the Apple SSO (Single Sign On) interacts with our platform.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Can I remove the Apple Single Sign On login option?**

No, this is the requirement set by Apple and cannot be changed.

![Thinkific White Label: A Guide to White Label Course Platforms 2026](https://letcheck.b-cdn.net/2422/clps6lt5905gion8z5rc38y33.jpg) [Courses](https://ezycourse.com/blog/category/courses/952) # Thinkific White Label: A Guide to White Label Course Platforms 2026 BY **Editorial Team** 9 Dec 2023 Thinkific is one of the hosted course platforms offering white-label branding in your online course. What’s white-label branding? White-label solutions offer customization options and allow you to establish a unique brand identity. No one would like to keep others' branding in their courses. Research suggests that consistent branding has the potential to boost [revenue by 20%](https://www.founderjar.com/branding-statistics/). Are you looking for the best white-label online course platforms? In this guide, I’ll let you know what Thinkific white label branding is and how to create your white label online course in 2026. In addition, I’ll suggest you a Thinkific alternative within budget - [EzyCourse](https://ezycourse.com/). Without further ado, let’s get started. ## **What is a White Label Online Course?** A white-label online course platform is a solution that enables you to create, customize, and sell courses under your brand. More interestingly, you can customize your brand's logo, design, and other branding elements. It will help you create a unique brand identity, and increase brand trust. to provide a seamless experience for your learners. You can create your white-label course with different course platforms like Thinkific. Let’s know what is Thinkific white-label branding and other alternatives to Thinkific. ## **What is Thinkific White Label Branding** ![](https://letcheck.b-cdn.net/2422/clps5vbae05coqh8z7i6411zj.png) Thinkific comes with different course creation options to help you create courses and communities. This platform stands out with its white-label branding options. It allows you to create a fully branded experience for your learners. Thinkific allows you to create a fully branded experience, including custom domains, logos, and colors. This helps in establishing a consistent brand presence throughout the learning journey. However, you have to go for the Grow plan of Thinkific, which comes at $159/month. ## **How to Remove Thinkific Branding** While you want to remove Thinkific branding from your site, you need to know where Thinkific tags are visible. Thinkific branding tags are found in specific sections of your website: - **Course Player:** Located at the bottom-left of the player. - **Community Area:** Positioned on the right sidebar within Thinkific Communities. - **Footer Area:** Displayed at the bottom-right section of your website. - **Email Notifications:** Featured after all emails sent to your students through Thinkific. We recommend removing Thinkific’s branding from these locations. To do this, follow the steps below: - **Step 1:** Go to the Settings menu - **Step 2:** Choose to Remove Thinkific branding - **Step 3:** Check off the box to the left of Remove Thinkific branding - **Step 4:** Click on the Save button Is Thinkific pricing too high for white-label branding? Are you looking for a [Thinkific alternative](https://ezycourse.com/blog/the-best-thinkific-alternative-in-2023)? Let’s check other online learning management systems. Know the 20 affordable [Thinkific alternative](https://ezycourse.com/thinkific-alternative) in 2026. ## **What are the Best Thinkific Alternatives for White Label Online Courses?** All the best online course platforms come with the option to remove their branding from your site. Some of the alternatives to Thinkific can be: 1. **Teachable** 2. **Kajabi** 3. [**EzyCourse**](http://ezycourse.com/) 4. **LearnWorlds** 5. **New Zenler** Now, let’s know how much you have to pay for white-label online courses. **Required Plan to Remove Branding in Online Learning Platforms:** | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Online Course Platform | White Label Branding/ Remove Branding | Required Plan | Pricing | | Thinkific | Remove Thinkific branding | Grow Plan | $149/m | | Teachable | Remove Teachable branding | Pro Plan | [$159/m](https://ezycourse.com/blog/teachable-pricing-which-plan-is-for-you) | | Kajabi | Remove Kajabi branding | Growth Plan | 159$/m | | LearnWorlds | Remove LearnWorlds branding | Learning Centre Plan | $299/m | | New Zenler | Remove New Zenler branding | Premium Plan | $197/m | | **EzyCourse** | **Complete white-label branding** | **Essential Plan** | [**$59/m**](https://ezycourse.com/pricing) | _Read more comparison:_[_Thinkific vs Teachable_](https://ezycourse.com/blog/thinkific-vs-teachable) As you see, EzyCourse comes with the most cost-friendly option for white-label branding in your course. Let’s know about the course platform in detail. ## **What is Ezycourse** ![](https://lh7-us.googleusercontent.com/Bz0m8xiNDKmQEEHZ5TGKrA-NPkx3poP-G3pINzv19O_5-Gc7gidfY2-HaEaZov2ura0aSHDYtH0CLcaXGJpJpII7TIr064xxbRESeAFLcLHGDDm2SzcE_A0rsOYTLk9TL6ef3vO2fFudp5mIdQM-SAA) EzyCourse is an all-in-one online course platform to simplify the course creation process. Unlike most other online course creation platforms, EzyCourse is not expensive to remove branding. You get a complete white-label branding of your web presence from their Essential plan starting at $59/month. Seems interesting? You should carefully consider these eight factors before making your choice: ### **1\. Customization and Branding:** Ensure the platform allows extensive customization to align with your brand. Look for options to use your brand logo, color, and domain name. A robust [white-label solution like EzyCourse](https://ezycourse.com/blog/your-ultimate-white-label-course-creation-platform) will seamlessly integrate with your brand identity. ### **2\. User-Friendly Interface:** Go for a platform with an intuitive interface. A user-friendly design is essential for both you as the online course creators and your students. Fortunately, EzyCourse offers a very intuitive [user interface](https://ezycourse.com/templates) to make course creation, management, and navigation super easy. ### **3\. White-Label Mobile Apps:** Check if the platform offers the option to create white-label mobile apps. This can enhance the user experience and contribute to the overall branding. EzyCourse is offering a [branded Android & iOS mobile app](https://ezycourse.com/feature/ezycourse-android-ios-app) at the Start plan ($99/m). ### **4\. Features and Functionality:** Check the features offered by the platform. Look for essential functionalities like course creation, assessment options, multimedia support, and other interactive features like chat options, real-time notifications, etc. Consider additional features such as analytics, marketing tools, and integration capabilities. ### **5\. Security and Data Privacy:** You should go for the platforms that prioritize security and data privacy. Ensure that the platform adheres to industry standards for data protection and offers features like secure payment gateways, encryption, and user authentication. You will get all these security options in EzyCourse. ### **6\. Dedicated Customer Support:** Dedicated support is a must for your assistance. If you go through the reviews of online training platforms, you will see plenty of frustrations for lack of support. In this case, EzyCourse comes with extremely dedicated support. You will get 24/7 reliable support for any troubleshooting issues and they also offer you a one-to-one meeting if necessary. ### **7\. Pricing:** As I have mentioned there are particular pricing plans in Thinkific to remove branding. Thinkific will take $149/m in the Grow plan for white-label branding. On the other hand, EzyCourse gives the white label branding at $59/m from their [essential plan](https://ezycourse.com/pricing). So, decide according to your suitability before committing financially. ### **8\. Customer Review:**     Read reviews and testimonials from other users. A platform with positive feedback indicates reliability and customer satisfaction. I must mention EzyCourse here for their 4.8-star customer review on Trustpilot. Let’s see a comparison chart of reviews and ratings on [Trustpilot](https://www.trustpilot.com/). | | | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Platforms | EzyCourse | Thinkific | Teachable | Kajabi | LearnWorlds | | Customer Review/ Rating (Trustpilot) | [4.8](https://www.trustpilot.com/review/ezycourse.com) | [4.4](https://www.trustpilot.com/review/thinkific.com) | [2.5](https://www.trustpilot.com/review/teachable.com) | [4.3](https://www.trustpilot.com/review/kajabi.com) | [3.5](https://www.trustpilot.com/review/www.learnworlds.com) | Keep all these factors in mind while selecting a white-label online course platform and ensure a branded learning experience for your audience. _Also Read: How to_[_Sell Online Courses_](https://ezycourse.com/blog/the-right-way-to-sell-online-courses-from-your-own-website)_from Your Own Website._ ## **Final Words: Which is the Best White-Label LMS?** Overall, creating an online course needs a lot of consideration.
